articles.title: THE BEST NEW FEATURES OF THE PIXEL 10 LINEUP
articles.summary: Google has announced the Pixel 10 lineup, introducing four devices with notable upgrades, including built-in Pixelsnap magnets for better wireless charging alignment and support for the faster Qi2 and Qi2.2 standards. The base Pixel 10 now includes a 5x telephoto lens, bringing high-end zoom to the entry-level model. The Pixel 10 Pro Fold boasts improved durability, while new AI features like Magic Cue and Camera Coach aim to enhance user experience through proactive suggestions and photography tips.

articles.title: IKEA'S MOST IKEA PRODUCT EVER
articles.summary: Ikea has partnered with Swedish designer Gustaf Westman to launch a 12-piece collection, headlined by a whimsical blue plate designed specifically to hold 11 meatballs in a neat row. Westman has described the design as a playful homage to Sweden's iconic holiday dish, aiming to blend function with humor by giving each meatball its own “throne.” The collection reflects both Westman's quirky style and Ikea's accessible aesthetic.

articles.title: ACROBAT STUDIO IS ADOBE'S NEW AI-POWERED HUB FOR PDFS
articles.summary: Adobe Acrobat Studio is an AI-powered PDF management hub that features PDF Spaces where users can upload up to 100 documents for AI analysis and collaboration. The platform, which initially only supports English, includes customizable AI assistants and integration with Adobe Express for image generation. Access starts at $25 per month.

articles.title: USER JOURNEYS VS. CUSTOMER JOURNEYS: WHY THE DISTINCTION MATTERS MORE THAN YOU THINK
articles.summary: User and customer journeys aren't interchangeable terms—users interact with products while customers pay money, and these roles often differ in SaaS and B2B models. Companies that design only for user delight without considering customer conversion risk create beloved products with no viable business model. The most successful products require mapping both journeys: user experiences that create engagement, and customer experiences that drive revenue.

articles.title: THE BEST DESIGN LEADERS CO-OWN METRICS, AND IT'S NOT AS SCARY AS YOU THINK
articles.summary: Designers must co-own business metrics, not by abandoning user-centered principles, but by translating design impact into outcomes executives understand. Demonstrate strategic value, build trust with decision-makers, and gain greater influence within organizations by aligning projects with Key Results, validating desirability, measuring design quality, and framing results in terms of revenue or cost savings.

articles.title: EASY WILL ALWAYS TRUMP SIMPLE
articles.summary: Complex systems in both biology and software often evolve by reusing what's already at hand, a process known in biology as exaptation and in software as choosing the “easy” solution. While simplicity is ideal, production pressures and human limits make complexity inevitable. Resilience comes from having diverse tools, partial knowledge across teams, and the ability to adapt systems collaboratively over time.

articles.title: BUILD WEBSITES EASILY
articles.summary: Clone any website to a full-stack app with EasySite AI. Enter a URL and its AI creates pixel-perfect React/Vue.js applications with backend functionality.

articles.title: UI CURATED EXPERIENCES FOR YOU INSPIRATION
articles.summary: Viewport UI is a curated collection that showcases diverse and inspiring user interface designs, from dashboard widgets to creative animations.

articles.title: CREATE VIDEOS BY CHATTING WITH AI
articles.summary: Create stunning social media content, product demos, and brand videos with Reeroll's professionally designed video templates.

articles.title: AND THE OSCARS BROADCAST GOES TO…YOUTUBE?
articles.summary: YouTube is reportedly in talks with the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences to broadcast the Oscars, potentially replacing Disney/ABC when their current deal expires in 2028. The move would align with YouTube's strategy of acquiring live event broadcasting rights, similar to its NFL Sunday Ticket package, though Oscar viewership has declined from 46 million in 2000 to 19.69 million in 2025. YouTube could leverage the opportunity to promote its creator talent, as several YouTubers have already transitioned into feature filmmaking with major distributors.

articles.title: HOW CREATIVES CAN DOUBLE DOWN ON THE SKILLS AI WILL NEVER REPLACE
articles.summary: Creatives can remain irreplaceable by mastering uniquely human skills that AI cannot replicate. These include reading people beyond data analysis, leveraging diverse life experiences for cultural understanding, and embracing messy and imperfect creative processes that break patterns. Building genuine relationships with clients and learning to partner with AI while maintaining human decision-making authority strategically is also essential.

articles.title: LISTEN UP FINANCE BROS, THIS IS HOW YOU NAIL A CRYPTO REBRAND
articles.summary: Crypto wallet Solflare has rebranded with Ragged Edge to position itself as the "Stronghold of the Free," combining security with bold, future-focused design. The identity uses gothic-inspired typography, vibrant colors, and modernized money-themed illustrations to welcome newcomers while resonating with seasoned crypto users, reframing crypto as both safe and innovative.

articles.title: THE ANATOMY OF A LIKEABLE POST: THE ART OF VISUALS AND CAPTIONS
articles.summary: A likeable social media post combines strategic visual elements and compelling captions that work together to capture attention and encourage engagement.

articles.title: HOW LEAH HAN'S ILLUSTRATIONS CELEBRATE MODERN FEMININITY
articles.summary: Illustrator Leah Han blends Eastern and Western influences to create tranquil yet powerful depictions of modern femininity, often showing women in moments of introspection and solitude.

articles.title: HOW VISION BECAME THE MOST OVERLOOKED DESIGN PROBLEM
articles.summary: Many companies confuse clarity with vision, resulting in polished execution but no shared sense of direction.
